title: potentilla inclinata var. oligotricha
intro: Potentilla inclinata var. oligotricha, also known as dwarf cinquefoil, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the mountainous regions of western North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Potentilla inclinata var. oligotricha have been reported to provide health benefits such as wound healing, antiviral activity, and immune system support. The plant is often prepared as a tincture or infused in hot water as a tea, and it can also be used in topical applications, such as salves and ointments, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
